title: Finding
description: A research finding produced by the surface agent and progressively enriched by the validator agent. Three distinct layers of fields track how far through the pipeline a finding has travelled.

linked_pr.title: LinkedPR
linked_pr.description: A GitHub pull request that references or attempts to fix the issue. Populated by the validator agent via `gh pr view`.

title.description: PR title as returned by the GitHub API.

url.description: Full URL of the pull request.

state.description: Current GitHub state of the PR. Use 'closed' for both merged and abandoned PRs; 'merged' field distinguishes them.

merged.description: True if the PR was actually merged into the target branch. A closed PR with merged=false is abandoned, not rejected.

merged_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the PR was merged. Null if not merged.

abandoned_reason.description: Explanation of why a closed-but-unmerged PR was not merged. Null when merged=true or state='open'. Key distinctions: ghstack artifact, superseded by other PR, author gave up, maintainer rejected.

reviews_summary.description: Narrative summary of review decisions: who approved, who requested changes, whether a maintainer triggered the merge bot, and the outcome.

maintainer_signal.title: MaintainerSignal
maintainer_signal.description: A structured signal extracted from a single maintainer or collaborator comment. Captures who said what kind of thing and the exact words. Populated by the validator agent.

author.description: GitHub username of the comment author.

role.description: GitHub authorAssociation of the commenter at the time of the comment. COLLABORATOR/MEMBER/OWNER are high-trust signals; CONTRIBUTOR and NONE carry less weight.

signal_type.description: The type of actionability signal this comment conveys. approved_fix: maintainer said fix is correct; requested_merge: maintainer asked bot to merge; blocked_action: maintainer said don't/can't act; wont_fix: explicit won't fix decision; by_design: behavior is intentional; needs_more_info: reproduction or detail required; scoped_bug: comment narrows the affected scope; partial_fix: comment indicates fix addresses only part of the issue.

quote.description: Direct verbatim quote from the comment, truncated to 200 characters. Must be an actual quote, not a paraphrase.

finding_id.description: LAYER 1 · SURFACE AGENT. Unique stable identifier for this finding. Format: {repo_short}_{issue_number} where repo_short uses underscores for slashes, e.g. 'pytorch_71774'. Must be stable across pipeline stages.

repo.description: LAYER 1 · SURFACE AGENT. Full repository name in owner/repo format, e.g. 'pytorch/pytorch'. Used to construct API calls in subsequent pipeline stages.

issue_number.description: LAYER 1 · SURFACE AGENT. GitHub issue number. Combined with repo to uniquely identify the issue.

issue_title.description: LAYER 1 · SURFACE AGENT. Issue title exactly as returned by the GitHub API.

issue_url.description: LAYER 1 · SURFACE AGENT. Full canonical URL of the GitHub issue.

issue_state.description: LAYER 1 · SURFACE AGENT. Current GitHub state of the issue. Note: OPEN does not mean unresolved — the issue may have been fixed without being closed.

issue_created_at.description: LAYER 1 · SURFACE AGENT. ISO 8601 timestamp when the issue was created. Used to detect staleness (pre-2023 open issues with no activity are likely stale).

issue_labels.description: LAYER 1 · SURFACE AGENT. All label names on the issue at time of fetch. Key labels to watch: 'module: correctness (silent)', 'skipped', 'actionable', 'triaged', 'needs reproduction'.

body_preview.description: LAYER 1 · SURFACE AGENT. First 500 characters of the issue body, for quick human scanning and classifier priming. Truncate at a word boundary if possible.

initial_classification.description: LAYER 1 · SURFACE AGENT. Surface agent's initial classification. correctness_silent: returns wrong answer without error; performance_regression: measurably slower than expected or than a prior version; missing_fast_path: correct but lacks an optimized code path; ci_noise: test-disable, flaky-test tracker, or CI housekeeping; maintainer_task: release, refactor, or infra task not suitable for contributors; unknown: does not fit any category.

surface_reasoning.description: LAYER 1 · SURFACE AGENT. 1-2 sentence explanation of why the surface agent flagged this issue and chose its initial_classification. Should name the specific signal (label, title pattern, body content) that triggered the flag.

oneOf.description: Array of linked PRs. Empty array means search was performed and none found.

linked_prs.description: LAYER 2 · VALIDATOR AGENT. PRs linked to this issue, found via `gh search prs` and `gh issue timeline`. Empty array means none found. Null means validator has not yet investigated. Validator must not change this after population.

oneOf.description: Array of structured signals from high-trust commenters. Empty array means no relevant signals found.

maintainer_signals.description: LAYER 2 · VALIDATOR AGENT. Structured signals extracted from COLLABORATOR, MEMBER, or OWNER comments on the issue and linked PRs. Empty array is valid when no high-trust signals exist. Null means not yet investigated.

oneOf.description: Each string is one specific reason this finding may not be actionable. Empty array means no blockers found — this is a positive sign.

blocking_signals.description: LAYER 2 · VALIDATOR AGENT. Specific reasons this finding may NOT be actionable by a contributor. Empty array means no blockers found. Null means not yet investigated. Examples: 'Maintainer explicitly said no real way to act on this', 'Issue is a CI test-disable tracker', 'Fix requires integrating external integer BLAS library'.

fix_scope.description: LAYER 2 · VALIDATOR AGENT. Estimated scope of the fix based on maintainer comments and linked PRs. single_function: one function in one file; single_file: changes confined to one file; single_module: changes within one PyTorch module directory; cross_cutting: changes span multiple subsystems; requires_external_lib: fix needs integrating a new dependency; unknown: cannot be determined from available evidence.

affected_hardware.description: LAYER 2 · VALIDATOR AGENT. Hardware platforms where the issue is observed. Free-form strings, e.g. ['cpu', 'a100', 'sm_89', 'consumer_gpu', 'rocm']. Empty array means hardware-independent or unknown. Null means not yet investigated.

still_reproducible.description: LAYER 2 · VALIDATOR AGENT. Validator's assessment of whether the issue can still be reproduced. confirmed: validator or recent commenter verified reproduction; likely: repro script exists, code path unchanged; unknown: old issue, no recent activity, code may have changed; unlikely: significant refactors since report, or a partial fix is known to have landed. Null means not yet assessed.

confidence.description: LAYER 3 · VALIDATOR AGENT. Confidence score from 0.0 to 1.0 that this finding represents an actionable bug a contributor could fix and get merged. Must be null when stage is 'raw'. Scores above 0.8 warrant file_pr or revive_abandoned_pr action. Scores below 0.3 warrant reject.

confidence_justification.description: LAYER 3 · VALIDATOR AGENT. Explicit step-by-step reasoning for the confidence score. Must cite specific evidence: label signals, maintainer quotes, PR merge status, reproducibility. Format: 'Step 1: ... (+/-). Step 2: ... Net: X.XX.'

recommended_action.description: LAYER 3 · VALIDATOR AGENT. Recommended next action. file_pr: write a new fix PR; file_issue: the finding warrants a new, better-scoped issue; revive_abandoned_pr: an existing approved-but-unmerged PR should be picked up; needs_human_review: validator is uncertain and a human should decide; reject: not actionable (noise, won't-fix, already resolved).

closest_ground_truth.description: LAYER 3 · VALIDATOR AGENT. Which ground truth case this finding most resembles. positive_71774: silent correctness bug with an approved-but-unmerged fix PR; noise_76962: CI housekeeping / test-disable tracker; ambiguous_72408: real issue but maintainers say no current path to a contributor fix.

ground_truth_delta.description: LAYER 3 · VALIDATOR AGENT. Concise description of how this finding differs from the closest ground truth case. Highlights the factors that make this finding more or less actionable than the reference.

oneOf.description: Array of topic label strings the user selected when this finding was generated, e.g. ["module: cuda", "module: linear algebra"]. Null for findings produced before the topics system was introduced.

selected_topics.description: LAYER 1 · SURFACE AGENT (optional). The topic labels the user selected for this pipeline run. Copied from data/selected_topics.json. Null for pre-topics-system findings.

corpus_version.description: LAYER 1 · SURFACE AGENT (optional). Which corpus version was used when this finding was generated. filtered_v1: original 1120-issue domain-filtered corpus; full_v1: 4870-issue full corpus (corpus_full.json). Null for pre-topics-system findings.

stage.description: PIPELINE DISCRIMINATOR · BOTH AGENTS. Current stage of this finding in the pipeline. raw: surface agent wrote Layer 1 only; enriched: validator populated Layer 2 and 3; validated: validator is confident in the verdict; needs_review: validator escalates to human; rejected: validator determined not actionable. Only the validator may advance stage past 'raw'.

created_at.description: LAYER 3 · SURFACE AGENT (sets on creation). ISO 8601 timestamp when this finding record was first written. Set once and never changed.

updated_at.description: LAYER 3 · VALIDATOR AGENT (updates on enrichment). ISO 8601 timestamp of the most recent modification to this finding record.

allOf.description: Enrichment rule: when stage is 'enriched' or beyond, all Layer 2 and Layer 3 fields are required and must be non-null.
Raw-stage rule: when stage is 'raw', confidence must be null if present. Prevents validators from pre-scoring without full investigation.
